channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Helpful or harmful? How to vet tax advice from social media
item.description: So you heard a piece of tax advice from a friend or on social media that sounds interesting. Should you try it? A certified public accountant explains how to vet the claim — and avoid getting scammed.
item.content:encoded: So you heard a piece of tax advice from a friend or on social media that sounds interesting. Should you try it? A certified public accountant explains how to vet the claim — and avoid getting scammed.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Giant robots battle it out in Detroit's Robowar
item.description: Fighting robots is a cultural fantasy going back at least to Richard Matheson's 1956 story "Steel." One Detroit impresario is now bringing the idea to the stage — and real audiences.
item.content:encoded:
Fighting robots is a cultural fantasy going back at least to Richard Matheson's 1956 story "Steel." One Detroit impresario is now bringing the idea to the stage — and real audiences.

(Image credit: Timothy Chen Allen)
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby

item.title: 5 ways to resist the urge to keep looking at your phone
item.description: So you want to spend less time on your phone. How do you do that when it's designed to suck you in? Life Kit spoke to experts in behavioral science, psychology and technology for real-world advice.
item.content:encoded: So you want to spend less time on your phone. How do you do that when it's designed to suck you in? Life Kit spoke to experts in behavioral science, psychology and technology for real-world advice.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Amid U.S.–China chip rivalry, open-standard designers warn their vision is at risk
item.description: The U.S. and China have been trying to dominate semiconductor design and manufacture. But the creators of one type of semiconductor design wanted to make it free for anyone in the world to build upon.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. and China have been trying to dominate semiconductor design and manufacture. But the creators of one type of semiconductor design wanted to make it free for anyone in the world to build upon.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Happy birthday to the telephone — oh, how you've grown in 150 years!
item.description: These days, our phones are basically extensions of our bodies. An MIT historian of science and technology takes us back to Alexander Graham Bell's famous first telephone call on March 10, 1876.
item.content:encoded: These days, our phones are basically extensions of our bodies. An MIT historian of science and technology takes us back to Alexander Graham Bell's famous first telephone call on March 10, 1876.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Shea

item.title: Old-school tricks and AI tech are weapons in the Iran war
item.description: Cyberwarfare is coming out of the shadows in the Iran war, from hacking phone apps to recruiting agents online to embracing AI as a weapon.
item.content:encoded:
Cyberwarfare is coming out of the shadows in the Iran war, from hacking phone apps to recruiting agents online to embracing AI as a weapon.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: New book recounts how Apple became a tech giant
item.description: In his new book "Apple: The First 50 Years," CBS Sunday Morning correspondent David Pogue tells the story of how Apple became one of the most valuable companies in the world.
item.content:encoded: In his new book "Apple: The First 50 Years," CBS Sunday Morning correspondent David Pogue tells the story of how Apple became one of the most valuable companies in the world.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Anthropic sues the Trump administration over 'supply chain risk' label
item.description: The Pentagon told suppliers they can't use Anthropic's artificial intelligence tools after the company said it would not let its tech be used for autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance.
item.content:encoded:
The Pentagon told suppliers they can't use Anthropic's artificial intelligence tools after the company said it would not let its tech be used for autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance.

(Image credit: Eva Marie Uzcategui and Julien de Rosa)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Trump is using immigration policy to suppress speech, lawsuit claims
item.description: A new lawsuit accuses the administration of violating the First Amendment by threatening the visas of researchers for work on disinformation and content moderation of social media.
item.content:encoded:
A new lawsuit accuses the administration of violating the First Amendment by threatening the visas of researchers for work on disinformation and content moderation of social media.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker, Janos Kummer and Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: OpenAI robotics leader resigns over concerns about Pentagon AI deal
item.description: A senior member of OpenAI's robotics team said guardrails around certain AI uses were not sufficiently defined before OpenAI announced an agreement with the Pentagon.
item.content:encoded:
A senior member of OpenAI's robotics team said guardrails around certain AI uses were not sufficiently defined before OpenAI announced an agreement with the Pentagon.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx
